name: Step Sequencer
description:
This algorithm is a 16-step note sequencer, based on the sequencers in the Expert Sleepers FH-2 module. It can output CVs and/or MIDI.

Each step has pitch, velocity, and a general-purpose “modulation” value. It also has a “Division”, which can either be a repeat count (the step is repeated a number of times on subsequent clocks) or a ratchet count (the step fires a number of gates within the duration of one clock). Each substep (either repeat or ratchet) can be on or off – this is set by the “Pattern” parameter. Furthermore each step or substep can be a tie – this is set by the “Ties” parameter.

A tie means a note which continues until just after the beginning of the next (sub)step. When outputting MIDI, this means the note off will follow the next note on i.e. the notes are played legato. When outputting CV, a tie means that the gate will not go low between the notes, and that the pitch CV will glide (if the glide time is non-zero).

The algorithm has a function for randomising the pitches, rhythm, or both.

Each Step Sequencer has internal storage for 32 sequences, which you can use like ‘snapshots’ or ‘patterns’ to prepare a number of sequences and then switch between them (manually, or under CV control) to build up songs.

Remember that every pitch etc. is a parameter and therefore mappable. Something as simple as an LFO modulating a sequence step’s pitch can produce fascinating evolving patterns. If you need to constrain the sequencer to a scale/key, follow it with a Quantizer.
gui_description: The display shows an overview of the 16 steps. The step pitches are displayed across the top of the screen. The current step being played is indicated by a highlight on the pitch value. The start and end steps are indicated by solid bars between the pitches. The step whose parameter is being edited is shown highlighted. The steps’ divisions, patterns, and ties are shown graphically. If the current parameter is a pitch, velocity, or modulation, these values are shown via the thin lines to the left of the steps. Divisions (repeats or ratchets) are shown as stacks of blocks: Ties are indicated by longer bars:

parameters.name: Sequence
parameters.description: Selects the current sequence.

parameters.name: Start
parameters.description: The first step to play. A reset will jump to this step.

parameters.name: End
parameters.description: The last step to play.

parameters.name: Gate length
parameters.description: Sets the length of the gate output if the type is “% of clock”.

parameters.name: Trigger length
parameters.description: Sets the length of the gate output if the type is “Trigger”.

parameters.name: Glide
parameters.description: Sets the glide time for tied notes.

parameters.name: Next sequence
parameters.description: Selects a sequence that will automatically play after this one, or ‘Off’ to stay on the current sequence.

parameters.name: Reset sequence
parameters.description: Selects the sequence that will play when the sequencer receives a reset signal, or ‘Off’ to stay on the current sequence.

parameters.name: Pitch
parameters.description: Sets the step pitch, as a MIDI note number.

parameters.name: Division
parameters.description: Sets the step repeat or ratchet count.

parameters.name: Pattern
parameters.description: Sets the step pattern – which substeps are on or off.

parameters.name: Ties
parameters.description: Sets the step tie pattern – which substeps are ties.

parameters.name: Velocity
parameters.description: Sets the step velocity.

parameters.name: Mod
parameters.description: Sets the step modulation value.

parameters.name: Mute
parameters.description: Sets the probability that the step will be muted.

parameters.name: Skip
parameters.description: Sets the probability that the step will be skipped.

parameters.name: Reset
parameters.description: Sets the probability that the step causes a reset.

parameters.name: Repeat
parameters.description: Sets the probability that the step will be repeated.

parameters.name: Randomise
parameters.description: When this parameter changes from 0 to 1, the sequence steps between the current start and end steps will be randomised according to the parameters that follow.

parameters.name: Min note
parameters.description: The minimum note pitch, if the distribution is “Uniform”.

parameters.name: Max note
parameters.description: The maximum note pitch, if the distribution is “Uniform”.

parameters.name: Mean note
parameters.description: The mean note pitch, if the distribution is “Normal”.

parameters.name: Note deviation
parameters.description: The note pitch deviation, if the distribution is “Normal”.

parameters.name: Min repeat
parameters.description: The minimum repeat count, if a step is a repeat.

parameters.name: Max repeat
parameters.description: The maximum repeat count, if a step is a repeat.

parameters.name: Min ratchet
parameters.description: The minimum ratchet count, if a step is a ratchet.

parameters.name: Max ratchet
parameters.description: The maximum ratchet count, if a step is a ratchet.

parameters.name: Note probability
parameters.description: The probability that a note will be on.

parameters.name: Tie probability
parameters.description: The probability that a step will be a tie.

parameters.name: Accent probability
parameters.description: The probability that a step will be accented.

parameters.name: Repeat probability
parameters.description: The probability that a step will be a repeat.

parameters.name: Ratchet probability
parameters.description: The probability that a step will be a ratchet.

parameters.name: Unaccented velocity
parameters.description: The velocity value to use for unaccented steps. Accented steps use velocity 127.

parameters.name: Pitch output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for the pitch CV output.

parameters.name: Gate output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for the gate output.

parameters.name: Velocity output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for the velocity output.

parameters.name: Mod output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for the modulation CV output.

parameters.name: Step output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for the step CV output.

parameters.name: Follow MIDI clock
parameters.description: Sets whether the sequencer follows MIDI clock.

parameters.name: Divisor
parameters.description: Sets the clock divisor when following MIDI clock.

parameters.name: MIDI channel (out)
parameters.description: Sets the output MIDI channel.

parameters.name: Output to breakout
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to the breakout.

parameters.name: Output to Select Bus
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to the Select Bus.

parameters.name: Output to USB
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to USB.

parameters.name: Output to internal
parameters.description: Enables internal MIDI output – that is, MIDI is sent to the other algorithms.

parameters.name: Mod CC
parameters.description: The MIDI CC to generate from the modulation lane.

parameters.name: MIDI channel (in)
parameters.description: Sets the input MIDI channel for assigning step pitches from MIDI notes.

parameters.name: Record velocity
parameters.description: If on, record velocity from input MIDI notes as well as the note number.

input_ports.name: Clock input
input_ports.description: The bus to use as the clock input.

input_ports.name: Reset input
input_ports.description: The bus to use as the reset input.

input_ports.name: Sequence CV input
input_ports.description: The bus to use to select the sequence.

input_ports.name: Sequence trigger input
input_ports.description: The bus to use for the sequence select trigger.

input_ports.name: MIDI Notes
input_ports.description: Note signals from MIDI used to program sequencer pitches.

input_ports.name: Clock input
input_ports.description: The bus to use as the clock input.

input_ports.name: Reset input
input_ports.description: The bus to use as the reset input.

input_ports.name: Sequence CV input
input_ports.description: The bus to use to select the sequence.

input_ports.name: Sequence trigger input
input_ports.description: The bus to use for the sequence select trigger.

output_ports.name: Pitch CV output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the pitch CV output.

output_ports.name: Gate output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the gate output.

output_ports.name: Velocity output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the velocity output.

output_ports.name: Modulation CV output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the modulation CV output.

output_ports.name: Step CV output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the step CV output (1/12th of a Volt per step).

output_ports.name: MIDI output
output_ports.description: MIDI output to breakout, Select Bus, USB, or internal algorithms.

output_ports.name: Pitch output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the pitch CV output.

output_ports.name: Gate output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the gate output.

output_ports.name: Velocity output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the velocity output.

output_ports.name: Mod output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the modulation CV output.

output_ports.name: Step output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the step CV output. This represents the current sequencer step number scaled as 1/12th of a Volt per step.
